channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ✈️🇮🇷 Iran Resumes Commercial Flights From Tehran Airport
item.description: Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport for the first time since the US-Israel conflict began two months ago, with flights operating to Istanbul, Muscat, Medina, and domestic destinations including Mashhad.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇪🇺🤝 Macron warns US not "fully reliable ally," urges European self-reliance
item.description: French President Macron said the US can no longer be seen as a fully reliable ally during a visit to Athens, calling for greater European autonomy in defense. The remarks came after a Pentagon memo revealed US plans to suspend Spain's NATO membership over its refusal to support the Iran war.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇨🇳🤝 Saudi Arabia looks to China and regional partners as US security guarantee wavers
item.description: The Iran war has exposed vulnerabilities in US security guarantees to Gulf allies, prompting Saudi Arabia to diversify partnerships with China, Pakistan, and Turkey. Riyadh has signed defense deals with Beijing and a mutual defense pact with nuclear-armed Pakistan.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷🇵🇰 Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip for Iran talks, saying US "holds all the cards"
item.description: President Trump abruptly cancelled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's trip to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, telling Fox News "you're not flying 18 hours to sit and talk about nothing." Iranian FM Araghchi left Islamabad without meeting US officials.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia-Ukraine Exchange Deadly Attacks as Zelenskyy Calls for More Talks
item.description: Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 3 and wounded 21 in Dnipro overnight. Both sides reported casualties in intensified fighting as Zelenskyy pushed for continued diplomatic engagement.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Warns US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Global Chip Supply Chains
item.description: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, warning of potential global supply chain disruptions.

item.title: 🇪🇺 France and Greece Say EU Defense Buildup No Alternative to NATO
item.description: President Macron stated the EU push for defensive capabilities is not meant to create a NATO alternative but to address a long-standing US request for Europe to do more, amid transatlantic tensions over the Iran war.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Hit by Wave of Coordinated Attacks from Armed Groups
item.description: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali capital Bamako and other cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇫🇷 Former NATO Chief Urges Britain and France to Lead New European Defense
item.description: A former NATO secretary general called for Britain and France to lead a new European security arrangement, warning the continent must be prepared to act without US support as transatlantic relations strain over the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Macron Reaffirms Efforts to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Energy Shortages
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron reiterated his focus on efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a day after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Cancels Envoys Trip for Iran Talks, Says We Have All the Cards
item.description: President Trump called off the trip of US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying too much time has been wasted on traveling.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Were Not Authorized for Local Operations
item.description: Mexicos government stated that two US federal agents killed in a car crash in the northern region were not authorized to participate in any local operation.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Drops Hint Its Next Aircraft Carrier May Be Nuclear-Powered
item.description: A video released for the PLA Navys anniversary uses wordplay suggesting a new nuclear-powered warship is under construction, potentially reshaping the Pacific naval balance.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Iran Foreign Minister Leaves Islamabad Without Meeting US Envoys
item.description: Irans foreign minister departed Islamabad before US envoys could arrive for talks, highlighting the collapse of Pakistans mediation efforts between the US and Iran.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan and Iran Leaders Discuss Regional Peace in Phone Call
item.description: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian about regional stability as diplomatic efforts continue to resolve tensions with the US. The call comes amid uncertainty over whether US envoys will travel to Islamabad for talks.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇴🇲 Iran FM Travels to Oman After Sharing Diplomatic Framework in Pakistan
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Oman from Islamabad where he presented a framework for ending the US-Iran conflict. Araghchi expressed skepticism about US commitment to diplomacy as Trump cancels envoy visit.

item.title: 🇲🇱⚔️ Coordinated Attacks Hit Multiple Cities Across Mali
item.description: Gunmen launched coordinated attacks against Malian military positions in the capital Bamako, Kati, Sevare, and northern areas in one of the largest assaults on the junta in years. The attacks targeted the international airport and military bases.

item.title: 🇭🇺🏛️ Hungary's Orban to Leave Parliament After Landslide Election Defeat
item.description: Viktor Orban announced he will give up his parliamentary mandate after losing to Peter Magyar in Hungary's election. The long-serving prime minister said he wants to stay on as Fidesz party leader while rebuilding the national side.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump Renews Call to End Filibuster Over SAVE America Act Impasse
item.description: President Trump called for eliminating the filibuster to pass the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act, which remains stalled in the Senate. The push highlights ongoing legislative gridlock on election-related legislation.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Explosions and gunfire reported in Bamako, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and Sevare as jihadist group JNIM and separatist Azawad Liberation Front stage simultaneous assaults. The UK Foreign Office has advised against all travel to Mali, and Bamako International Airport has been temporarily closed.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Macron says EU defense clause is stronger than NATO Article 5
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron declared during a visit to Greece that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is stronger than NATO Article 5 because it leaves no option for non-compliance. The statement comes as Trump questions NATO and US commitment to European defense.

item.title: 🇻🇪 US eases sanctions to let Venezuela pay Maduro legal fees
item.description: The US Department of Justice modified sanctions to allow the Venezuelan government to cover legal fees for former president Nicolas Maduro, who faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. Maduro defense lawyers had argued that blocking payment violated his right to counsel.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism during Africa tour
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said he will continue speaking out against war despite criticism from President Trump, who attacked the pontiff for opposing US Iran policy. The Pope began an 11-day tour of Africa amid escalating Vatican-Washington tensions over the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 US senator urges China participation in nuclear arms control
item.description: Senator Deb Fischer, chair of the Senate Armed Services strategic forces subcommittee, said China must be involved in any nuclear arms control talks. China has repeatedly refused to join US-Russia nuclear agreements, seeking parity before negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Cancels US Envoys' Trip to Pakistan for Iran Talks
item.description: President Donald Trump cancelled a planned trip by US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran war mediation talks, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call.' Iran's foreign minister had already left Islamabad, dealing a setback to peace prospects.
